text.title: earned-value-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for earned-value-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring early CPI warnings because "it will recover".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CPI rarely improves on its own — the "CPI cliff" is empirically documented. A CPI of 0.80 at 20% complete predicts a final CPI of 0.70-0.85 in most projects.

failure-mode.description: Re-baselining to "fix" red metrics without CCB approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hides true variance. A project that has re-baselined three times has three sets of lies as its history. Future forecasts become meaningless.

failure-mode.description: Reporting SPI/CPI numbers without a 3-period trend line.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A single-period metric value tells you nothing about direction. Stakeholders need trajectory, not a snapshot. Always show the last 3 periods together.

failure-mode.description: Running both MegaLinter and the underlying native tool (e.g., ruff) twice in the same CI workflow because "the agent fixes faster from the SARIF".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Effort-driven scopes (consulting hours) can show CPI greater than 1 while the deliverable rots. Hours × rate; must pull rate-per-role.
